content:
- Updated the task file to `<passes>true</passes>`, checked off the acceptance criteria and execution plan, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks*.md`.
- Committed all workspace and Ralph-state changes as `task finished 01-task-scaffold-rust-workspace-and-dependency-policy: scaffold Rust workspace foundation` and pushed commit `5cacb53` to `master`.
- Next: quit immediately; the next turn should start by reading the new progress log and choosing the next task.

- Read prior progress log entry and loaded the list-active-tasks skill instructions.
- Started refreshing Ralph task summaries and inspecting workspace files to confirm the actual stack before choosing the next task.
- Next: read current_tasks/current_tasks_done and choose-task rules, resolve blockers, then write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and quit.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and confirmed the repository is a Rust workspace with a runner runtime crate and a source-bootstrap CLI crate.
- Checked current_tasks/current_tasks_done plus task files for bug, priority, blocked_by, and meta-task markers; none were present among active tasks, so there are no visible blockers or higher-priority bug tasks.
- Next: set .ralph/current_task.txt to story-02-rust-foundation/02-task-build-single-binary-container-contract.md and quit immediately.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with the selected next task path for the remaining Rust foundation work.
- Verified the current_task file now points at the single-binary container contract task.
- Next: quit immediately so the next turn can execute that task using the tdd skill.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-02-rust-foundation/02-task-build-single-binary-container-contract.md`; next I am checking whether it is still in planning or ready for execution.
- Next: read the task file, inspect repo structure, then either create/update the required plan and quit or start TDD execution.

- Determined this is a planning turn: the selected task file is still `not_started` and had no `<plan>` entry.
- Read the operational model, recommended design, test strategy, existing runner config/tests, and confirmed `/usr/bin/docker` is available, so the next turn can validate a real container build/start contract instead of a fake file-only contract.
- Next: create the task plan under the story-specific `_plans` directory, link it from the task file, mark the plan `NOW EXECUTE`, and stop immediately for the execution turn.

- Created `.ralph/tasks/story-02-rust-foundation/02-task-build-single-binary-container-contract_plans/2026-04-18-single-binary-container-contract-plan.md` and linked it from the active task file.
- The plan now captures the TDD execution order for direct binary startup, mounted config conventions, real Docker build/run contract tests, README novice-user docs, and a final improve-code-boundaries cleanup pass; it ends with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: in the next turn, read the plan, execute it one red-green slice at a time, then finish only after `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Read prior Ralph progress, confirmed the active task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-02-rust-foundation/02-task-build-single-binary-container-contract.md`.
- Loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions for this turn.
- Next: read the task file and linked plan, determine whether to execute or revert to planning.

- Read the active task and linked plan; confirmed the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this is the execution turn.
- The plan requires red-green execution across startup contract, mounted config convention, Docker build/run contract, docs, and all repository lanes.
- Next: inspect runner CLI/config/tests/README/design docs, then start slice 1 with a failing integration test.

- Started TDD execution. Added a failing integration assertion for `runner run --config` to expose the actual config file path in the public startup contract.
- Greened slice 1 by updating the startup summary to print `config=<path>` alongside postgres/webhook/tls/reconcile details.
- Hit a temporary Cargo archive assembly error for `librunner.rlib`; verified it was a stale-artifact issue because a verbose rebuild succeeded without source changes.
- Next: add the mounted-config convention test, then refactor the duplicated config-summary boundary if the next slice exposes it.

- Finished the main implementation slices: direct startup now reports the config path, mounted-config validation reports config+toplevel TLS contract, the repo has a root Dockerfile plus .dockerignore, and the long-lane test now performs real `docker build` + direct `docker run` validation/startup.
- Updated README with the novice-user Docker quick start and flattened config/runtime boundaries by introducing `LoadedRunnerConfig` plus central TLS label formatting.
- Also cleared the workspace `target/` once after repeated stale-object linker/archive failures; clean sequential reruns were then stable.
- Next: run `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` sequentially, then update the Ralph task/plan state only if every lane is green.
